summary: onCall Cloud Function that creates a carrier company (vendor doc) and provisions its first admin user. Creates vendors/{vendorID}, users/{uid}, and vendor_users/{vendorID}/users/{uid} atomically. Used to onboard new carrier companies onto the LogiTruck platform.

businessIntent.problem: Carrier companies must be provisioned server-side: the vendorID container must be created before any user can be associated to a company, and the admin user's auth account + Firestore profile must be created atomically to avoid orphaned accounts.
businessIntent.expectedOutcome: Calling createCarrier produces a verified vendor doc at vendors/{vendorID}, a Firebase Auth account for the admin, and dual-written profile at users/{uid} + vendor_users/{vendorID}/users/{uid}. Returns vendorID and adminUID to the caller.

selectedBuildingBlocks.name: idempotent-event-processing
selectedBuildingBlocks.reason: getUserByEmailSafe check prevents duplicate Firebase Auth account creation if called twice with the same adminEmail

selectedBuildingBlocks.name: firestore-data-model-access
selectedBuildingBlocks.reason: Function writes to vendors/{vendorID}, users/{uid}, and vendor_users/{vendorID}/users/{uid} — all three collections require correct path and field knowledge

existingPatternsToFollow: Auth check via request.auth?.uid as first statement
getUserByEmailSafe from functions/core/user.js for idempotent user lookup
Dual-write to users/{uid} and vendor_users/{vendorID}/users/{uid} with merge:true
knownAntiPatternsToAvoid: data.auth (v1 onCall pattern — always use request.auth)
Creating Firebase Auth user without checking if email already exists first
Writing to vendor_users/{vendorID} parent doc — it must remain an empty container
Calling admin.initializeApp() inside the function file

dataModelsTouched.notes: Primary write — creates the vendor company doc. vendorID = uuid(). Fields: vendorID, name, searchKeywords, serviceCategoryIDs, status, createdBy, createdAt, updatedAt
Admin user global profile. merge:true to handle existing accounts. Fields: id, email, firstName, lastName, vendorID, role, rolesArray, createdAt
Admin user scoped to vendor. path: vendor_users/{vendorID}/users/{uid}. Mirror of users/{uid} for vendor-scoped queries. merge:true

input.companyName: string — display name of the carrier company
input.adminEmail: string — email for the first admin user
input.adminFirstName: string — first name of the admin
input.adminLastName: string (optional) — last name of the admin

output.vendorID: string — the generated UUID for the new vendor
output.adminUID: string — Firebase Auth UID of the admin user

sequence: 1. Validate request.auth?.uid — throw HttpsError('unauthenticated') if missing
2. Validate required inputs: companyName, adminEmail, adminFirstName — throw HttpsError('invalid-argument') if any missing

4. Write vendors/{vendorID} with { vendorID, name: companyName, searchKeywords: [companyName.toLowerCase()], serviceCategoryIDs: [], status: 'active', createdBy: authUID, createdAt: serverTimestamp(), updatedAt: serverTimestamp() }
5. Call getUserByEmailSafe(adminEmail) from functions/core/user.js — returns existing Firebase Auth user or null
6. If no existing user: call admin.auth().createUser({ email: adminEmail, displayName: adminFirstName + ' ' + adminLastName })
7. Write users/{uid} with merge:true — { id: uid, email, firstName, lastName, vendorID, role: 'carrier', rolesArray: ['carrier'], createdAt: serverTimestamp() }
8. Write vendor_users/{vendorID}/users/{uid} with merge:true — same profile fields
9. Return { success: true, vendorID, adminUID: uid }
functionSpec.idempotencyNotes: getUserByEmailSafe prevents duplicate Firebase Auth account on retry. vendors/{vendorID} uses uuid so re-call creates a new vendor — caller must not retry without a new vendorID.
functionSpec.errorHandling: unauthenticated for missing auth, invalid-argument for missing inputs, internal for unexpected Firestore/Auth errors wrapped in try/catch

create.description: onCall function: validates auth + inputs, creates vendor doc, provisions admin user via Firebase Auth, dual-writes user profile to users/ and vendor_users/
Jest tests: unauthenticated call, missing fields, duplicate email (idempotency), successful creation verifying all 3 Firestore writes

modify.reason: Register createCarrier export at the INSERT_FIREBASE_FUNCTION marker

avoid: Real Firebase Auth calls in tests
Integration tests that require the Firebase emulator

expectedPassingSignals: All 4 test cases pass (unauthenticated, invalid-argument, idempotent email, successful creation)
No require errors — all imports resolve
No admin.initializeApp() call inside the function file
failureEscalationRules: If getUserByEmailSafe import fails: check functions/core/user.js exports
If uuid import fails: verify uuid is in functions/package.json dependencies
If test mock structure fails: load testing-guide building block and regenerate test

steps: Validate: node require check + jest run
Produce handoff with integration step: register export in functions/index.js

claudeCodeIntegrationPlan.reason: Cloud Functions require Claude Code to: register export in functions/index.js, verify getUserByEmailSafe import path, confirm no hardcoded keys, run emulator smoke test
steps: Review generated createCarrier.js — confirm auth check is first statement
Confirm getUserByEmailSafe is imported from correct path (functions/core/user.js)
Confirm vendors/{vendorID} fields match data-model-registry.json spec
Add export to functions/index.js at INSERT_FIREBASE_FUNCTION marker
Run jest tests from functions/ directory
Run Firebase emulator smoke test with test payload
runtimeValidation: Firebase emulator: call createCarrier with valid payload, verify vendors/{vendorID} doc exists
Firebase emulator: call with same adminEmail twice, verify second call reuses existing UID
finalCommitChecklist: No hardcoded API keys or secrets in generated files
jest passes for createCarrier test suite

acceptanceCriteria: Unauthenticated call throws HttpsError('unauthenticated') before any Firestore read
Missing companyName, adminEmail, or adminFirstName throws HttpsError('invalid-argument') naming the missing field
Successful call creates vendors/{vendorID} with correct fields including serviceCategoryIDs: []
Admin user Firebase Auth account is created if email is new; existing account is reused if email already exists
users/{uid} and vendor_users/{vendorID}/users/{uid} are both written with merge:true
Function returns { success: true, vendorID, adminUID }
All 4 jest test cases pass in the sandbox
